skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit AI images through the inference.sh belt CLI using models such as GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and Topaz.
skill.summary: Run text-to-image, image editing, stitching, and upscaling jobs through inference.sh image apps.
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are true positives because the skill instructs agents to run the network-backed belt CLI and npx skill installation commands. The network URL findings are expected for documentation and example image inputs, and the weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 appears to be a false positive with no cryptographic code present. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Network-Backed External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The external command findings are true positives. The skill directs agents to run belt commands that submit prompts and image URLs to inference.sh applications. This is expected for the skill, but it can transmit user-provided content to remote model providers.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit belt invocations for remote image apps. The behavior is core functionality, not covert execution.

medium_findings.title: Commands That Install Related Skills
medium_findings.description: The related-skills section includes npx skills add commands. These are documented examples, but running them can modify the local skill environment and pull code from a remote source.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install commands are plainly shown in a bash block. They are not hidden, but they would change local tool state if executed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs
low_findings.description: The network findings are true positives for documentation links, a hosted preview image, and placeholder image input URLs. They do not show credential exfiltration or hidden callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or example inputs. No evidence found that secrets are collected or sent to attacker-controlled endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Appears False Positive
low_findings.description: The static high finding at line 3 does not correspond to cryptographic code. The line is metadata text describing supported image models and use cases.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains descriptive front matter, not hashing, encryption, signing, or password handling. I found no cryptographic implementation in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples for Remote Image Jobs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains bash examples for belt app run and npx skills add. These patterns require user awareness because they can execute local commands and contact remote services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple bash blocks contain explicit commands. The risk is operational exposure, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Image teams need quick access to many generation models without rewriting commands. This skill provides ready-to-run belt CLI prompts for image creation, editing, stitching, and upscaling.
actual_capabilities: Runs text-to-image jobs with GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and related models.
Shows image editing commands that pass source image URLs and editing prompts to supported apps.
Lists image application discovery commands through the belt CLI.
Provides examples for upscaling and stitching remote image inputs.
Links to inference.sh documentation for running apps and image generation workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Sends prompts and image URLs to remote services when commands are executed.
Does not include local image generation models or offline rendering.
Model availability, pricing, and output quality depend on inference.sh and provider services.

use_cases.title: Product Mockup Creation
use_cases.description: Generate polished product photos, background variants, and quick visual directions from prompt text.

use_cases.title: Campaign Visual Exploration
use_cases.description: Compare several image models while preparing concepts for ads, social posts, and landing pages.

use_cases.title: Prototype Asset Production
use_cases.description: Create concept art, stitched references, and upscaled visuals during early product or game prototyping.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a clean studio product photo for a ceramic coffee mug. Use a square aspect ratio and neutral lighting.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Choices
prompt_templates.scenario: Select a suitable model before generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend which listed model I should use for a cinematic landscape with accurate text on a sign. Then prepare the belt command.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Image-to-image editing with a source URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an image editing command that changes the background of this product photo to a beach at sunset. Use this image URL: https://example.com/product.jpg

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Visual Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a workflow that generates three hero image concepts, stitches the best two references horizontally, and upscales the final result.

output_examples.input: Create a product photo of black running shoes on a white studio background.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a belt command for a high-quality image model and includes the prompt, quality setting, and expected remote execution step.

output_examples.input: Change my uploaded jacket photo to a rainy city background.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects an image editing app, includes the source image URL field, and writes a concise editing prompt.

output_examples.input: Make a wider preview from two generated images and improve resolution.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a stitching command followed by an upscaling command for the combined image.

best_practices: Review prompts and source image URLs before running belt commands.
Avoid sending private, regulated, or unreleased assets to remote model providers.
Confirm model cost, resolution, and licensing terms before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated CLI commands without checking the model name and input payload.
Do not include credentials, customer data, or confidential metadata in prompts.
Do not assume remote model outputs are cleared for trademarked or regulated use.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It prepares and runs belt CLI commands that call inference.sh image applications.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it support image editing?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for editing images by passing a source image URL and a prompt.

faq.question: Can it upscale generated images?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Topaz upscaler example through the belt CLI.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: Commands can send prompts and image URLs to remote services, so users should avoid sensitive content.

faq.question: Is the skill blocked from publishing?
faq.answer: No. The audit found medium operational risk, but no confirmed malicious intent.
